items: Who changed what?
When did this bug appear?
How to undo last week's mistake?
How do two people edit the same file?

title: SVN vs. Git: Centralized vs. Distributed
desc: Why the industry moved from Subversion to Git

body.alt: SVN centralized vs Git distributed architecture

items: SVN: single central server — commit, history, diff all require network access
Git: every developer has the full repository — work offline, commit locally, sync when ready

title: What Is Git?

items: Distributed: every developer has the full history locally
Created 2005 by Linus Torvalds for the Linux kernel
Industry standard: used by 97%+ of professional developers

desc: The tool that answers: who changed what, when, and why?

title: Git's Core Concept: Snapshots
desc: Git doesn't track file changes. It takes snapshots of your entire project.

body.code: Commit 1 Commit 2 Commit 3 +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ | App.java | | App.java* | | App.java* | | Calc.java | | Calc.java | | Calc.java | | | | Test.java | | Test.java*| +-----------+ +-----------+ +-----------+ (* = changed)
body.note: Each commit = permanent record: Who, When, What, Why

body.code: Working Directory Staging Area Repository (your files) (prepared) (permanent) edit files -----> git add ---------> git commit "my desk" "outbox" "sealed archive"

items: Working directory: files as you see them
Staging area: changes selected for next commit
Repository: permanent history of all commits

body.code: # Create repository git init # Check state (use this CONSTANTLY) git status # Stage a file git add Calculator.java # Commit git commit -m "Add Calculator class with basic operations" # View history git log --oneline
body.note: Demo rule: Run git status after every single operation.

items: Navigate to your Calculator project from Module 0
git init, then git status
git add pom.xml, then git status — what changed?
git add . (stage everything)
git commit -m "Initial project structure"
git log — what do you see?
Change the add() method, then git diff
Stage and commit again, check git log --oneline

body.quote: Run git status after every step!

body.code: # Undo last commit but KEEP your changes git reset --soft HEAD~1 # Unstage a file (keep changes) git restore --staged Calculator.java # Discard changes in a file (DANGEROUS!) git restore Calculator.java

body.quote: Warning: git restore without --staged destroys your changes. No undo.

title: AI-Augmented: Claude Code Understands Git State

body.code: You: What changed since my last commit? Claude Code: Since commit a3f2d1c "Add divide method", you have 2 modified files: src/main/java/Calculator.java Line 24: added modulo() method (new) Line 8: changed return type of divide() from int to double src/test/java/CalculatorTest.java Lines 31-45: added 3 new test methods for modulo Unstaged: Calculator.java (staged: CalculatorTest.java)
body.note: Claude Code runs git diff, git status, and git log behind the scenes, then explains the result in human language.

body.code: # Bad commit messages: "fix" "update" "stuff" "WIP" "asdf" # Ask Claude Code: You: Commit my changes with a good message # Claude Code reads the diff and suggests: "Add input validation to divide() — throw IllegalArgumentException on zero divisor"

You: Review my staged changes before I commit. Claude Code: Reviewing 2 staged files... Calculator.java:18 — WARN divide() catches ArithmeticException but logs nothing. If this fails silently, debugging will be impossible. Fix: add log.error("Division by zero", e); Calculator.java:25 — ERROR modulo() does not handle b == 0. Same bug as divide() had before you fixed it. Fix: add the same zero-check guard. CalculatorTest.java — OK 3 new tests, all have assertions, edge cases covered.
body.note: This catches issues that CheckStyle and PMD miss — logical bugs, missing guards, silent failures.

body.quote: Run this before every commit. It takes 10 seconds and saves hours of debugging.

items:
Make 3 separate changes to your Calculator:
  • Add a modulo() method
  • Fix a bug in divide()
  • Add a comment explaining the class
For each: ask Claude Code to review and suggest a commit message
Make 3 separate commits (not one big commit!)
git log --oneline — your history should tell a clear story

body.quote: Rule: Read every suggested message. Modify if inaccurate.

items: main: always stable, always works
feature branch: isolated work on one task
merge: bring finished work back to main

body.code: git checkout -b feature/add-modulo # work, commit, work, commit... git checkout main git merge feature/add-modulo

items: WRONG: git checkout main → edit → commit — main is now broken for everyone
RIGHT: git checkout -b feature/my-task → edit → commit → merge back to main
Rule: Never commit unfinished work to main

title: Interactive: Visualize Git with D3
desc: Try git commands and see what happens to the commit graph in real time.

body.card: Try these commands in the playground:
items: git commit — watch a new node appear
git branch feature — see a new branch pointer
git checkout feature — watch HEAD move
git commit (on feature) — branches diverge
git checkout main then git merge feature — see the merge
git reset HEAD~1 — watch a commit disappear

title: git stash: Save Work Without Committing
desc: You're mid-task on a branch but need to switch. Stash saves your changes temporarily.

body.code: # you're editing on feature/login, but need to fix a bug on main git stash # saves uncommitted changes, working dir is clean git checkout main # switch safely # fix the bug, commit git checkout feature/login git stash pop # restores your saved changes

items: git stash — save and clean working directory
git stash pop — restore and remove from stash
git stash list — see all stashed changes
git stash drop — discard a stash you no longer need

title: Merge Conflicts: Don't Panic
desc: A conflict happens when two branches change the same line.

items: <<<<<<< HEAD = your current branch version
======= = divider
>>>>>>> feature/... = incoming branch version
Git cannot decide which is correct — you must decide

body.code: You: I have a merge conflict in Calculator.java. Help me. Claude Code reads the file and finds: <<<<<< HEAD public double divide(double a, double b) { if (b == 0) throw new ArithmeticException("Division by zero"); ======= public double divide(double a, double b) { return a / b; >>>>>> feature/calculator-v2 Claude Code: Your branch added a zero-check guard. The feature branch doesn’t have it. Keep the zero-check — removing it would reintroduce a division-by-zero bug. Suggested resolution: keep HEAD version (yours).
body.note: Claude Code explains why one version is better — it doesn’t just pick randomly. You still make the final decision.

body.code: # IDE files .idea/ *.iml .vscode/ # Build output target/ *.class # NEVER commit secrets .env *.key credentials.json api-tokens.txt

body.quote: Critical rule: Once a secret is in Git history, it stays FOREVER.

items: Make sure main branch is clean and working
Create branch feature/power — add a power() method
Commit on this branch
Switch back to main, change the divide() method
Commit on main
Try: git merge feature/power
If conflict: use Claude Code to help resolve it

body.quote: Goal: You must experience a merge conflict at least once.

title: Pull Requests: Code Review Before Merge
desc: In professional teams, nobody merges directly. Every change goes through review.

body.code: # 1. Push your feature branch git push -u origin feature/add-modulo # 2. Open a Pull Request on GitHub # Title: "Add modulo operation" # Reviewer checks your code # 3. After approval: merge into main # 4. CI pipeline ran before merge — broken code never reaches main

items: Every change is reviewed before it reaches main
Reviewer catches bugs, style issues, design problems
CI pipeline runs before merge — broken code blocked
Full history of who approved what (audit trail)

title: Remote Repositories: Working with a Team

body.code: # Connect to remote git remote add origin https://github.com/user/calculator.git # Push your commits git push -u origin main # Get latest changes git pull # Clone existing project git clone https://github.com/team/project.git
Your machine ---- git push ----> Remote (GitHub) <---- git pull ---- Colleague

items: Git = snapshots of your project over time
Core cycle: edit → add → commit → push
Branches isolate work. Merges integrate it.
Conflicts are normal — not errors
.gitignore protects secrets and junk
Every commit = an audit trail entry with provenance
Claude Code augments Git: explains state, writes commits, reviews changes, resolves conflicts
